chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What is electric current?
questions.answer: Electric current is the flow of electric charges.

questions.question: What is an electric circuit?
questions.answer: An electric circuit is a closed path through which current flows.

questions.question: Name a source of electric current.
questions.answer: An electric cell is a source of electric current.

questions.question: What is a battery?
questions.answer: A battery is a combination of two or more cells.

questions.question: What is a conductor?
questions.answer: A conductor is a material that allows electric current to pass through it.

questions.question: Give an example of a conductor.
questions.answer: Copper is an example of a conductor.

questions.question: What is an insulator?
questions.answer: An insulator is a material that does not allow electric current to pass through it.

questions.question: Give one example of an insulator.
questions.answer: Rubber is an example of an insulator.

questions.question: What is a switch?
questions.answer: A switch is a device used to open or close an electric circuit.

questions.question: What is a fuse?
questions.answer: A fuse is a safety device that melts when excessive current flows.

questions.question: What is the heating effect of electric current?
questions.answer: It is the production of heat when electric current flows through a conductor.

questions.question: Name one device that works using heating effect.
questions.answer: Electric iron.

questions.question: What is the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.answer: It is the production of a magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?
questions.answer: An electromagnet is a magnet produced by passing electric current through a coil.

questions.question: Name a material used as the core of an electromagnet.
questions.answer: Soft iron.

questions.question: What happens to a compass near a current-carrying wire?
questions.answer: The compass needle deflects.

questions.question: Name one device that uses an electromagnet.
questions.answer: Electric bell.

questions.question: Why are wires covered with plastic?
questions.answer: Plastic acts as an insulator and prevents electric shocks.

questions.question: What happens when a circuit is open?
questions.answer: Current does not flow.

questions.question: What happens when a circuit is closed?
questions.answer: Current flows through the circuit.

questions.question: Name one appliance that produces heat using electricity.
questions.answer: Electric heater.

questions.question: What is the function of connecting wires?
questions.answer: They allow electric current to flow in the circuit.

questions.question: Why is a fuse wire thin?
questions.answer: It melts quickly when excessive current flows.

questions.question: What type of magnet is an electromagnet?
questions.answer: A temporary magnet.

questions.question: Name one device that produces light using electricity.
questions.answer: Electric bulb.
